item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Deceptively engrossing. Zigs when you expect it to zag. Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is an evocative piece, and has a lot of potential… but I feel like the events are a little too unconnected (even though I got what you were going for) and I think the numerous notes spell it out a little too clearly. I wonder if it wouldn’t be better without the last two notes and keeping the phone call and the last addendum as clues

Some lines that stuck out for me:

composed of a black substance similar to fire.

I’m not sure what this means, the beings were made from fire? If so, how were they terminated?

Anomalous vehicles were demolished.

Why wouldn’t the foundation examine these vehicles for any evidence of the anomalous?

After this, the plane reanimated.

“reanimated” makes it sound like the plane came to life… might suggest “continued its flight path” instead.

It is unknown if the passengers were aware of this event.

Wouldn’t the researchers have interviewed the passengers? Couldn’t they easily find out if the passengers were aware or not?

item.title: Image Credits
item.content:encoded:
Here's all the images I used:

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Easter_Sunday_bushfire_smoke.JPG

https://unsplash.com/photos/P4OJ6w32v-Y

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Melbourne_(AU),_View_from_Eureka_Tower,_Queens_Bridge_--_2019_--_1481.jpg

https://unsplash.com/photos/bIpBXIRD3I4

All other images in this article were created by me. All edits made to these images are my work unless specified as otherwise.

item.title: Well-put together. +1
item.content:encoded:
I like this article - The image at the end really does tie it all together - each event being a state of grief I assume this Michael (Reality bender living there, perhaps?) was going through after losing this Desiree. The best event imo showing this is what I assume to be the Depression stage - the rain.

However; the interview portion really did feel like blatant filler. Occam's razor really is key when writing SCP articles in my opinion - Adding length just for length/something irrelevant to the proper 'story' makes any article tedious and boring. However, the rest of the article was entertaining and mostly relevant to the ending; of which you pulled off surprisingly well. If you can pull this off with other articles, I'll be sure to look forward to them. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I'm glad you enjoyed it.

I actually thought it was obvious what was going on, but I guess it would feel like that from my perspective. While Gee's crit (as always) is a tad harsh, it has brought up a lot of stuff I didn't think of, and that's helped me with the drafts I've been writing since this. Sometimes crit is unforgiving.

By the way, I won't explain too much so as to let you interpret this however you want, but I will say that there is a substantial link between the event logs and the image at the end.

item.title: Huh. +1
item.content:encoded:
This one felt like 5999, in that most of the events felt completely unrelated, but somehow, in some way, they are related. I honestly have no idea what happened, but I liked the story and the mystery. It's pretty good for a first skip!
I think gee0765 is wrong about this article. I don't think that the conclusion comes out of left field. Given that each event seems unrelated, it would make sense that the concluding parts seem unrelated as well, and there IS a reason for it. I don't know what that reason is.
I did not understand it, maybe I mistranslated it, can you explain it to me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The majority of this is painfully obtuse, which makes it near-impossible to get anything out of it beyond the same shallow story about loss from the notes that we've seen dozens of times before. The interview especially is almost all just filler content, and the ending with the file manipulation and image comes entirely out of left field, making it feel disjointed and somewhat bolted on as a conclusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I appreciate it. I'm definitely going to be writing more, so keep an eye out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is your first? Holy crap, I thought it was incredible! Good job, man!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not yet. This is my first SCP! I know I still have a lot to improve on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Understandable. Have you made any other works?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I wanted to write something based on event logs and figured the Extranormal Events page was too restrictive for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this SCP! It’s interesting although I feel this would better fit under the “Extranormal Events” category, but that’s just my opinion.
